## About You

## Your Submission

**What I Built:**
<!-- Brief description of the page you created -->
I created a prototype webpage that reimagines the future of the F!rosh website. While it is currently a template, it lays the groundwork for more interactive features I plan to implement, such as dynamic schedule cards that flip to reveal event descriptions and more robust data integration. To deepen my familiarity with React and experiment with advanced CSS markup, I also built and styled fully functional "About Us" and "Contact Us" pages.

**Challenges I Faced:**
<!-- What was tricky? What took longer than expected? -->
Translating my design vision into CSS proved to be the biggest hurdle. Structuring the layout pattern for the schedule and perfecting the top and bottom banners required a significant amount of time and trial-and-error. I relied heavily on documentation and AI tools to troubleshoot styling quirks and align the elements exactly how I envisioned them, which was a time-consuming but incredibly rewarding learning process.

**What I'm Most Proud Of:**
<!-- Your highlight — a feature, a design choice, a problem you solved -->
I am incredibly proud of the final page layout and user experience. Initially, I crammed all the schedule data onto a single, overwhelming page. By leveraging Gemini, I refactored the UI to use a clean, tabbed navigation system that lets users toggle between different dates for a much cleaner layout. While my early attempts at adding a background pattern felt too distracting and heavy, I’m excited to use my design skills in tools like Canva, Adobe Illustrator, or Lucidpress to create a custom, polished background asset to replace the current minimalist gray.

## 🤖 AI Usage Declaration

Please check one:

- [Yes] I used AI tools (ChatGPT, Claude, Copilot, etc.)
- [ ] I did not use AI tools

*If you checked yes, please fill in the fields below. If no, feel free to skip.*

**Tools Used:**
<!-- e.g. ChatGPT 4o, GitHub Copilot, Claude Sonnet -->
Gemini

**How I Used Them:**
<!-- e.g. "Generated the initial CSS layout, helped debug a React state issue, explained how useEffect works" -->
While I used Google for the basic structure of the CSS layout, I really couldn't get the right layout for the schedule table. So I used Gemini mostly in how to effectively style the table using CSS. 

**What I Reviewed / Changed After:**
<!-- e.g. "Rewrote the filter logic myself after the AI version didn't handle edge cases, adjusted all colours to match the brand" -->
In the beginning I had 5 seperate tables that displayed the schedule. With Gemini's help, I changed the layout so that I can display one table at a time using buttons. This helped optimized my web page even more. 

**Reflection:**
<!-- What did you learn from the parts you built yourself vs. the parts AI helped with? A few honest sentences is all we're looking for. -->
While I am an expert in Java and working on the basic HTML structure. It was my first time using CSS and markup styles. However, I learned that it was much easier to debug this, becasue there was a lot of videos/documentations that goes through the process step-by-step. Google Gemini was a great help in understanding my vision and trying to optimize it to look better on the website.
